title: brassica oleracea var. bullata
intro: Brassica oleracea var. bullata, commonly known as wild kale or broad-leaved kale, is a variant of the cabbage plant that has broad, flat leaves with a waxy texture. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer. The leaves of the Brassica oleracea var. bullata plant are rich in vitamins, minerals, and fiber, providing several health benefits including improved digestion, reduced inflammation, and enhanced immune function. In herbal preparations, the leaves can be consumed raw in salads or cooked as a vegetable, or made into teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
